mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) relative to baseline during periods of stable low rates, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0%, weak labor market conditions suppress consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.0%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and capital expenditure recovery, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to predict positive returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show negative or zero correlation with industrial production growth, or the signal directional accuracy falls below 50% in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.3% signals accelerating inflation that compresses real corporate margins and prompts market repricing, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in >55% of months where CPIAUCSL rises above 0.3%, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm broad-based labor market tightening, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal predicts negative returns in >50% of triggered months, or the interaction coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in rolling regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product confirms macroeconomic expansion, which supports corporate earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns in >60% of quarters, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below chance during expansionary phases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% indicates severe supply-side contraction and weakening economic activity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in >55% of months where industrial production falls below -0.5%, or the threshold signal shows no statistical edge (Rank IC < 0.02) in walk-forward tests.
